structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: roth_2026_eventstudy_note is listed as a working paper, but verification finds a 2026 Japanese Economic Review article; update venue metadata.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: Exhibit 9.1 posits R_SA, R_CS, and R_W rather than deriving C(pi,Sigma,G,T) from the four published estimator definitions and Section 6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The advertised Omega/frontier variance object is computed only from the ad hoc witness matrices, not from a worked derivation of Z_m, L_m, and V for the named estimators.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: C stacks only SA, CS, and W contrasts against the EIF; the iff also needs an explicit BJS-EIF equality assumption or a BJS contrast row.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Once L_m is defined as the IF loading, 'same IF iff (L_m-L_EIF)tau=0' is mostly a loading-definition unfold unless C receives an independent primitive characterization.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The norm convention for ||C_m tau||_{Omega_m} is dimensionally ambiguous because Omega_m is defined as C_m V C_m^T while C_m tau is already the contrast-coordinate vector.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add C_BJS=L_BJS-L_EIF to the stacked frontier or state BJS=EIF as a named assumption/theorem, then restate the iff against the all-four contrast.
Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a derivation of Z_m, L_m, C_m, and Omega_m from the published BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge formulas for the T=4 instance.
Give an independent primitive characterization of C tau=0, such as a rank/row-space condition in pi, Sigma, G, T, and a, so the frontier is not merely the definition of loading equality.
Update the Roth 2026 bibliography/checklist entry to the Japanese Economic Review publication and keep the working-paper/arXiv version only as an alternate version if needed.

recommended_next_step: Revise the constructive exhibit and the BJS-EIF condition before another novelty pass; the kernel is not flagship-ready while C and Omega are not derived from primitives.
